source: Schacke H, Docke WD, Asadullah K. 2002 Pharmacol Ther; Kale VP et al. 2022 Toxicologic Pathology; Greaves P. Histopathology of Preclinical Toxicity Studies (4th ed) ch.11; Boumpas DT et al. 1993 Ann Intern Med
description: Expected findings for glucocorticoids (dexamethasone, prednisone, prednisolone, hydrocortisone, methylprednisolone). Class effects driven by glucocorticoid-receptor (GR) activation: HPA-axis suppression, lymphocyte apoptosis, hepatic gluconeogenesis induction, fluid retention. The profile is unusually broad because GR is expressed in essentially every nucleated cell. BW-up at HD is regulatorily adverse despite being the canonical fluid-retention pharmacology (cross-references compound-class-flags.json:glucocorticoid).

expected_findings.description: Body-weight gain from fluid retention + fat redistribution
expected_findings.severity_threshold: Sustained gain with edema or Cushingoid features = adverse
expected_findings.rationale: GR activation in renal mineralocorticoid-receptor-shared aldosterone-cleared pathway drives sodium + water retention; truncal fat redistribution is a downstream metabolic effect. Schacke 2002 review.
Hyperphagia is a recognized component of glucocorticoid-induced metabolic syndrome (Boumpas 1993).
GR-driven transcription of PEPCK, G6Pase, and other gluconeogenic enzymes; insulin resistance via IRS-1 phosphorylation.
Glucocorticoids inhibit neutrophil egress from circulation and reduce adhesion-molecule expression, releasing the marginated pool. Predictable acute-on-chronic finding.

expected_findings.severity_threshold: Severe lymphopenia (>80% drop) = adverse for chronic dosing
expected_findings.rationale: GR-driven thymocyte apoptosis + redistribution to lymphoid tissues. Concurrent eosinopenia. Greaves ch.11.
Profound eosinopenia is a sensitive biomarker of glucocorticoid action.

expected_findings.severity_threshold: Severe atrophy with concurrent immunosuppression-related findings = adverse
expected_findings.rationale: Thymocyte apoptosis is the canonical glucocorticoid signature. Cross-references C7 corroboration trigger MI_atrophy_same_organ_same_dose_sex.

expected_findings.description: Adrenal cortical atrophy from HPA-axis suppression
expected_findings.rationale: Exogenous glucocorticoid suppresses ACTH, leading to cortical atrophy (zona fasciculata + reticularis preserve zona glomerulosa).
Histopathologic correlate of OM_adrenal_down. Reverses on washout.
Glucocorticoid-induced antidiuretic-hormone antagonism + osmotic diuresis from hyperglycemia.
